title: Exact results for the Hubbard model on bipartite lattices in spatial dimensions $d>1$: Seven theorems from the full [SU(2)$\times$SU(2)$\times$U(1)]/$\mathbb{Z}_2^2$ symmetry
abstract: There are few exact results for the Hubbard model on bipartite lattices of spatial dimension $d>1$. Nevertheless, the Hubbard model with transfer integral $t$ and onsite repulsion $U$ on bipartite lattices with $N_a$ sites, such as the square, honeycomb, cubic, body-centered cubic, face-centered cubic, and diamond lattices, provides the simplest toy model for describing electronic correlations in many condensed-matter systems and is therefore a quantum problem of considerable physical interest. Seven exact theorems that provide new physical insight into the model are established. Overall, the exact framework based on physical spins and physical $η$-spins for the Hubbard model on bipartite lattices of spatial dimension $d>1$ introduced in this paper offers a robust foundation for future studies of the model, as well as of the condensed-matter materials, such as cuprate superconductors, graphene and graphene-derived systems, and other quantum systems, that it describes.
summary: This paper provides a rigorous mathematical framework for the Hubbard model on bipartite lattices in dimensions greater than one. By identifying a hidden U(1) symmetry, the author establishes seven exact theorems that describe the ground state's behavior, such as the absence of level crossings and the suppression of certain charge fluctuations. These results offer a robust foundation for understanding electronic correlations in materials like graphene and cuprate superconductors. The work also demonstrates how the model reduces to the t-J model in the strong-coupling limit.
main_problem: The paper aims to establish exact results for the Hubbard model on bipartite lattices in dimensions d > 1 by utilizing its full [SU(2) x SU(2) x U(1)]/Z2^2 global symmetry, specifically addressing the previously ignored hidden U(1) symmetry.
main_result: The author establishes seven exact theorems regarding the ground state properties, including the absence of level crossings in symmetry-resolved sectors, the absence of quasiparticle double occupancy/empty sites, and the decomposition of spin-charge entanglement.
method: The study employs an exact quasiparticle representation via a u-unitary transformation and utilizes the Perron-Frobenius theorem and analytic perturbation theory to prove ground state properties.
model_or_system: The Hubbard model on bipartite lattices (such as square, honeycomb, cubic, BCC, FCC, and diamond) with transfer integral t and on-site repulsion U.
key_observables: Spin S_s, eta-spin S_eta, tau-translational U(1) generator S_tau, energy and momentum eigenstates, and spin/eta-spin currents.
important_parameters: Interaction strength u = U/t, number of lattice sites N_a, and the presence of external magnetic fields (h) or chemical potential (mu).
main_assumptions: The results are derived for bipartite lattices in spatial dimensions d > 1 and specifically focus on the regime of finite U > 0.
paper_structure: The paper begins by identifying the full global symmetry and introducing a quasiparticle representation, followed by the derivation of seven specific theorems regarding ground state properties, and concludes with an analysis of the large-u limit and the mapping to the t-J model.
analysis_basis: full PDF text, analyzed in chunks
